description.task: Implement a JarManifestBuilder class that constructs a MANIFEST.MF file content for an executable JAR. The manifest defines the main class, classpath entries, and build metadata. Validate that all required entries are present before building.
instructions: mainClass(), addClasspathEntry(), and attribute() all return 'this' for fluent chaining
build() prepends 'Manifest-Version: 1.0' as the first line
Class-Path line uses a space separator between entries
Additional attributes are appended after the standard entries in insertion order
build() throws IllegalStateException('Main-Class is required') if mainClass is null or blank
description.hint: The implementation is mostly provided. Ensure attribute() does attributes.put(key, value) and returns this. The build() method body is complete in the skeleton.
description.expectedOutput:
Manifest-Version: 1.0
Main-Class: com.example.Application
Class-Path: lib/spring-core.jar lib/jackson.jar
Built-By: Maven
Implementation-Version: 1.0.0

description.task: Create a DeploymentConfigManager class that manages application configurations for different environments (dev, staging, production). Each environment has specific settings (DB URL, log level, replicas). Implement validation that production configs meet minimum requirements.
instructions: registerConfig() stores the config using environment name as key
getConfig() retrieves the config by environment name, returns null if not found
validateProduction() checks: replicas >= 2, tlsEnabled==true, logLevel in {WARN, ERROR}
Each violation is a descriptive string (e.g., 'Production requires at least 2 replicas')
validateProduction() returns empty list when all checks pass
description.hint: In validateProduction(): if (prod.replicas() < 2) violations.add('Production requires at least 2 replicas'); if (!prod.tlsEnabled()) violations.add('TLS must be enabled in production'); if (!Set.of('WARN','ERROR').contains(prod.logLevel())) violations.add('Log level too verbose for production');
description.expectedOutput:
Dev replicas: 1
Violations: 2
Production requires at least 2 replicas
Log level too verbose for production

description.task: Implement a HealthCheckService class that simulates Spring Boot Actuator's /actuator/health endpoint. Register health indicators (database, message broker, disk space) and compute an overall application health status as UP, DOWN, or DEGRADED.
instructions: checkAll() invokes all registered indicators and returns their results
overallStatus() iterates over all indicator results
If any component has Status.DOWN, return Status.DOWN immediately
If all are UP but at least one is DEGRADED, return Status.DEGRADED; otherwise return Status.UP
description.hint: In overallStatus(), call checkAll() then use stream: if any DOWN return DOWN; else if any DEGRADED return DEGRADED; else return UP. Use anyMatch(d -> d.status() == Status.DOWN).
description.expectedOutput:
Components: 3
Overall: DEGRADED
Redis status: DEGRADED
